title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Class 8 — Karnataka State Board
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A Bengaluru street vendor buys mangoes at ₹60 per dozen and sells them at ₹7 per mango. What is his profit percentage per dozen?
questions.explanation: SP per dozen = 7 × 12 = ₹84. CP = ₹60. Profit = ₹24. Profit% = (24/60) × 100 = 40%. Every small business in Karnataka — from Mysuru flower vendors to Mangaluru fish sellers — uses this exact profit margin calculation. Your Chapter on Profit and Loss (Commercial Arithmetic) IS business accounting.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Karnataka's KSRTC bus fare from Bengaluru to Mysuru increased by 12% after a fuel hike, and then again by 8% due to infrastructure levy. If the original fare was ₹250, what is the final fare?
questions.explanation: After 12% increase: ₹250 × 1.12 = ₹280. After 8% increase on ₹280: ₹280 × 1.08 = ₹302.40. Successive percentage changes require compound calculation — not simple addition. This is why petrol price hikes and tax-on-tax (like GST on already-taxed goods) use this compounding principle from your Percentage chapter.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A Dharwad farmer deposits ₹25,000 in a Grameen Bank account at 5% simple interest per annum. After how many years will his amount become ₹31,250?

options.text: 5 years — using SI formula T = (SI × 100) / (P × R)
3 years — dividing interest by rate only

questions.explanation: SI = Amount - Principal = ₹31,250 - ₹25,000 = ₹6,250. T = (SI × 100) / (P × R) = (6250 × 100) / (25000 × 5) = 625000/125000 = 5 years. Grameen banks and Self Help Groups (SHGs) in Karnataka's rural areas — especially Bellary, Bidar, Kalaburagi districts — use simple interest for micro-loans. Your SI formula directly helps rural families evaluate loan terms.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A textile mill in Hubballi gives workers a dearness allowance (DA) that is 35% of their basic salary. If a worker's basic salary is ₹12,000/month, and they pay 10% as PF (Provident Fund) on the total salary (basic + DA), what is their monthly PF contribution?
questions.explanation: DA = 35% of ₹12,000 = ₹4,200. Total salary = ₹12,000 + ₹4,200 = ₹16,200. PF = 10% of ₹16,200 = ₹1,620/month. PF is a retirement savings scheme — your employer also contributes 12%. Over 30 years of work, with compound interest at ~8.1% p.a. (EPFO rate 2024), your ₹1,620/month becomes approximately ₹27 lakh! Understanding your salary slip requires your Percentage chapter.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Karnataka's crop insurance scheme (PM-FASAL BIMA) charges a premium of 2% of the sum insured. A Vijayapura farmer insures his groundnut crop for ₹80,000. If his actual loss is ₹65,000, what is his net recovery after paying the premium?
questions.explanation: Premium = 2% of ₹80,000 = ₹1,600. Claim = ₹65,000 (actual loss, within sum insured). Net recovery = ₹65,000 - ₹1,600 = ₹63,400. Crop insurance is critical for Karnataka farmers (especially in drought-prone Kalaburagi, Raichur, Vijayapura districts). Your Percentage and Profit-Loss chapters help farmers evaluate whether insurance is worth buying — a life-changing financial decision!
questions.subject: Mathematics
